lines should be limited to 120 characters. This can be achieved like this:

                       change = (
                            data_dic[slice_name + "_" + cell_id + "_SM.PDUSessionSetupFail.0"][-1]
                            / data_dic[slice_name + "_" + cell_id + "_SM.PDUSessionSetupReq.01"][-1]
                            - data_dic[slice_name + "_" + cell_id + "_SM.PDUSessionSetupFail.0"][-2]
                            / data_dic[slice_name + "_" + cell_id + "_SM.PDUSessionSetupReq.01"][-2]

Possibly missing:
Is there a trace id? principal id?
(Are they in mdcfmt under another name?)
status code? (values: SUCCESS, INPROGRESS, FAIL_WARN, FAIL_ERROR, FAIL_FATAL)
this USER line appears to be redundant

python3

also, why not write the code to use unbuffered or line buffered output?

with python 3.7, try
sys.stdout.reconfigure(line_buffering=True)

another possibility is
sys.stdout = io.TextIOWrapper(open(sys.stdout.fileno(), 'wb', 0), write_through=True)    

(some other suggestions can be found at
https://fedingo.com/how-to-disable-output-buffering-in-python/ 
https://stackoverflow.com/questions/107705/disable-output-buffering
https://blog.finxter.com/what-is-python-output-buffering-and-how-to-disable-it/

and many other pages)

I think the below 2 lines were missed in the successive patchsets. This is the bug-fix I had mentioned few weeks back(to convert message to json and add header details in post message). Please add the below 2 lines

json_object = json.dumps(Post_Message, indent = 4)
response = requests.post(pathToDmapTopic, json_object, verify=False, headers={"Content-Type":"application/json"})

do NOT pass in something like IsUnitetestPath. Use mocks & fakes instead within Test modules.

This requirement lead to prepare bulk data to contain all the Topics data in a iterative manner, It is difficult to refactor against ML Implementation for the topic used to get the dmaap config data against each cell.

DMaaP Message Router is deprecated and should not be used for any new code.
Please use kafka native to interact with strimzi kafka in ONAP.
https://docs.confluent.io/kafka-clients/python/current/overview.html
Or alternatively use the Strimzi Kafka Bridge REST api.
https://strimzi.io/docs/bridge/latest/

Will convert the existing DMaaP api into Kafka Producer API to update the predicted result into policy engine. Remove this Ddaap Service.

PM Get Data Topics replaced with Kafka API Consumers

Is this file still needed?
Can we remove all refs to dmaap?

Thank you. Much appreciated.
Regarding the relevant topics, this is a question for the topic "owners".
From what I can see, in ONAP, there is little or no information regarding the topics being used in the system and what they are being used for/contain.
From what I can see the PERFORMANCE_MEAS topic is published to by dcae-pm-mapper.

Note: If this new mS is to use kafka native, the new OOM helm chart will require the necessary Strimzi Kafka User with the appropriate acls to access the required topics. We can sync up on this when the new chart is being added later.
Ideally we should remove any references to dmaap.
Not sure if this is planned to be run as a standalone mS in ONAP but if so the Producer and Consumer conf will need to be extended to support additional security/authentication params.

As per my understanding, It is not planned to run in standalone mode. currently it is interacting with external Kafka consumer and producer related to PERFORMANCE_MEASUREMENTS and Policy Topics. When we try to add any sasl_mechanism we can see error messages at Kafka service. So currently i believe security/ authentication are not implemented at external ends.

In ONAP we are using Strimzi kafka with the following security requirements for all clients.
sasl.mechanism=SCRAM-SHA-512
security.protocol=SASL_PLAINTEXT

for java apps, the following

sasl.jaas.config=org.apache.kafka.common.security.scram.ScramLoginModule required username="user" password="pass";

For python, not sure.
Something along these lines maybe:

 'sasl.username': SASL_USERNAME,
 'sasl.password': SASL_PASSWORD,

There exist certain issue from python library, 
Results in SCRAM-SHA-512 mechanism handshake failed: Broker: Request not valid in current SASL state: broker's supported mechanisms

refered https://github.com/confluentinc/confluent-kafka-python/blob/master/examples/sasl_producer.py

following code used
-------------------
SECURITY_PROTOCOL = "SASL_PLAINTEXT"
SASL_MECHANISM = "SCRAM-SHA-512"
SERVERS = "kafka:9092"
SASL_USERNAME = "admin"
SASL_PASSWORD = "1243"

conf = {

 'bootstrap.servers': SERVERS,
 'sasl.mechanism': SASL_MECHANISM,
 'security.protocol': SECURITY_PROTOCOL,
 'sasl.username': SASL_USERNAME,
 'sasl.password': SASL_PASSWORD,
 'group.id': "222",
 'auto.offset.reset': 'smallest' 
 }
producer = Producer(conf)
testData = "test"
producer.produce('PERFORMANCE_MEASUREMENTS', value=testData.encode('utf-8'))
producer.poll(1)
